patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/449

## Problem (contract drift)
`pm contracts --json` advertises extension_contracts.policy_modes = ["off","warn","enforce"] (same for trust_modes), but governance config keys (e.g. governance-workflow-enforcement) accept `strict`, not `enforce`. Agents driving config from contracts construct invalid writes — exactly the drift class pm contracts exists to prevent.

## Proposed fix
Single source of truth for mode tokens shared by contracts and config validation; either rename config token to `enforce` (with alias) or fix contracts to advertise `strict`; add a drift spec that walks advertised enum tokens through the real validators.

## Definition of done
Advertised policy/trust mode tokens are accepted verbatim by config set; drift spec pins contracts<->validator parity; contracts:update run.
GH-449: contracts policy_modes advertises 'enforce' but governance config accepts 'strict' (contract drift)

Dogfood GH-449: pm contracts advertises policy_modes [off,warn,enforce] but config validation requires 'strict' — agents driving config from contracts hard-fail. Duplicate check: searched policy modes/enforce strict — no existing item.
Advertised policy/trust mode tokens are accepted verbatim by pm config set; drift spec walks every advertised enum through the real validator; contracts:update run

author: maintainer-agent
message: Contract-vs-runtime drift class: fix should derive policy_modes from the same contract source of truth
